name: Politics & News
description: Political figures, global news, and media

questions.q: Who was the first President of the United States?
Who was the Soviet Premier during the 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis?

source.notes: Opposite John F. Kennedy in the 13-day standoff.

questions.q: Jimmy Carter became U.S. President in which decade?

source.notes: Inaugurated January 1977; served one term.

questions.q: The United Nations headquarters is located in which city?

source.notes: On an 18-acre site in Manhattan, on international territory.

questions.q: Julia Gillard became the first female Prime Minister of which country in 2010?

source.notes: Led the Australian Labor Party; served 2010–2013.

questions.q: Juan Perón was the charismatic three-time President of which South American country?

source.notes: President 1946–1955 and again 1973–1974.

questions.q: Robert Walpole, who took office in 1721, is generally considered the first what?

source.notes: Served about 20 years — longest UK PM tenure to date.

questions.q: Bashar al-Assad became President of which Middle Eastern country in 2000, succeeding his father Hafez?

source.notes: Regime toppled in December 2024 by a rebel offensive led by HTS.

questions.q: Before entering politics, Vladimir Putin spent 16 years working for which Soviet organization?

source.notes: Joined the KGB in 1975; stationed in Dresden, East Germany.

questions.q: The Irish republican party Sinn Féin was founded in 1905 by which politician?

source.notes: Name means 'We Ourselves' in Irish.

questions.q: What is the name of the British Prime Minister's official country residence in Buckinghamshire?

source.notes: Used by Prime Ministers since David Lloyd George in 1921.

questions.q: Which UK political party, led by Margaret Thatcher, formed a government in 1979?

source.notes: Also known as the Tory Party.

questions.q: The Arabic-language international news channel Al Jazeera was launched in which year?

source.notes: Headquartered in Doha, Qatar.

questions.q: François Hollande was elected President of which country in 2012?

source.notes: Socialist Party; defeated Nicolas Sarkozy.

questions.q: In October 2013, which country experienced a 16-day federal government shutdown?

source.notes: October 1–16, 2013; centered on disputes over the Affordable Care Act.

questions.q: Typhoon Haiyan, one of the deadliest tropical cyclones ever recorded, struck which country in November 2013?

source.notes: Killed over 6,300 people; devastated Tacloban City.

questions.q: Kim Jong-un succeeded whom as North Korea's supreme leader in 2011?

source.notes: Kim Jong-il, his father, died December 17, 2011.

questions.q: Which 2015 UK General Election was won by the Conservative Party under David Cameron?

source.notes: Gave Cameron his first majority government.

questions.q: Juan Perón's beloved second wife, a First Lady who inspired a musical, was known affectionately as what?

source.notes: Eva 'Evita' Perón (1919–1952); died of cervical cancer at age 33.

questions.q: Former Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi also owned which famous European football club?

source.notes: Owned AC Milan 1986–2017; won 8 Serie A and 5 Champions League titles under him.

questions.q: Who is the Australian-born media mogul who founded News Corp?

source.notes: Owns The Wall Street Journal, The Sun, and many others; Fox News originally a News Corp property.

questions.q: Juan Perón ruled Argentina from 1946. His original political movement, centered on labor and nationalism, is known as what?

source.notes: Continues to be a dominant political force in Argentina today.
